title: Finite Policy Iteration

solver_reuse.target: finite policy-iteration bad policy-value QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact finite replay solves the first policy-evaluation linear system to V(s2) = 2/3 from the committed MDP table, while a malformed row claims 1/2, isolating a compact scalar Farkas conflict beneath the policy-iteration trace.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional dynamic-programming rows only when they add distinct modified-policy-iteration, undiscounted/average-reward, linear-programming-formulation, stochastic-approximation/Q-learning, or numerical-honesty pressure; keep policy-iteration termination and optimality theorems, the policy-improvement theorem in general, and floating-point dynamic-programming claims in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed finite MDP table, every committed policy, each policy-evaluation linear-system solution, every improvement round's Q-values and greedy argmax, the termination-by-stability round, and the componentwise monotone value improvement as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route the false policy-evaluation claim through a source-linked QF_LRA artifact and checked Farkas evidence after exact replay solves the conflicting value equation.
Expose the pack through probability, dynamics, optimization, policy-iteration, exact-vs-floating, and learner-query surfaces.

Keep the policy-improvement theorem in general, policy-iteration termination and optimality theorems, average-reward and continuous MDP theory, stochastic approximation, and floating-point dynamic programming marked theorem or numerical-honesty horizon.
